title: Revisiting Professional Forecasters’ Past Performance and the Outlook for 2026
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Professional forecasters expect 1.9% GDP growth and 2.9% CPI increase for 2026. The forecasts for 2025 have been accurate. The Philadelphia Fed's SPF survey pro
excerpt: Professional forecasters expect 1.9% GDP growth and 2.9% CPI increase for 2026. The forecasts for 2025 have been accurate. The Philadelphia Fed's SPF survey provides detailed economic projections for 2026-2029.

content.introduction: Professional forecasters expect 1.9% GDP growth and 2.9% CPI increase for 2026. The forecasts for 2025 have been accurate. The Philadelphia Fed's SPF survey provides detailed economic projections for 2026-2029.
body: System wide Board of Governors Atlanta Boston Chicago Cleveland Dallas Kansas City Minneapolis New York Philadelphia Richmond St. Louis San Francisco. # Fed in Print. Bank Series Content Type Author JEL Classification. # Revisiting Professional Forecasters’ Past Performance and the Outlook for 2026. **Abstract:**  So far, the 2025 forecasts have turned out to be rather accurate. For 2026, professional forecasters expect 1.9% GDP growth and a 2.9% increase in the consumer price index. ## Access Documents. **File(s):**  File format is text/html. ## Authors. ## Bibliographic Information. **Provider:**  Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis. **Publication Date:**  2025-12-18.

![Image 1](https://www.stlouisfed.org/on-the-economy/2025/dec/professional-forecasters-past-performance-outlook-2026)</>. Despite these challenges, the forecasts we described in a December 2024 [blog post](https://www.stlouisfed.org/on-the-economy/2024/dec/professional-forecasters-performance-2025-economic-outlook) turned out to be quite accurate based on the data available today. Gascon, ldquo[Revisiting Professional Forecasters’ Past Performance and the Outlook for 2026](https://www.stlouisfed.org/on-the-economy/2025/dec/professional-forecasters-past-performance-outlook-2026),rdquo _St. Louis Fed On the Economy_, Dec. 18, 2025.
